macro_segments.title: Opening discussion on Safek Gerushin and the status of Tzaras Ervah

micro_segments.title: Review of yesterday's case: Safek Kiddushin and Safek Gerushin in the Mishna
The distinction between true Safek Gerushin and Psul Gerushin (rabbinically invalid divorce)
How Chazaka (presumed status) applies when there is doubt about divorce

macro_segments.title: Bringing the Gittin case of thrown get and its application to Yevamot

micro_segments.title: The Mishna in Gittin about a get thrown and landing equidistant from husband and wife
Two pairs of contradictory witnesses versus abstract doubt and the concept of Safeka D'Oraisa
The principle that contradictory witnesses cancel each other and Chazaka is restored
The Bar Shatya property case illustrating how contradictory witnesses restore Chazaka

macro_segments.title: Resolving the apparent contradiction: why Mishna excludes true Safek Gerushin if Chazaka applies

micro_segments.title: Abaye's interpretation: learn one case from the other (Kiddushin and Gerushin are parallel)
Rava's distinction: each case has unique Psulim that don't apply to the other
Why the Mishna specifies cases: Zeman is unique to Gerushin, not Kiddushin

macro_segments.title: Why Zeman (date requirement) applies to Get but not to Shtar Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Two reasons for requiring Zeman in Get: Peirot (fruits of property) and protecting from accusations of adultery
Why Zeman is not required in Shtar Kiddushin: Arusah has no Peirot rights
The pragmatic reason: majority of Kiddushin use Kesef, not Shtar, so requirement would be unfair
Slaves can be acquired both ways and Zeman is required; but Kiddushin custom differs
Practical problem: if Shtar Kiddushin has no Zeman, the document can be lost or destroyed
Why Zeman is still kept for Get despite the same document loss problem: Get is needed as proof of divorce status
Technical discussion: witnesses cannot testify from a written document (Mipichem v'lo mipi Ksavam)

macro_segments.title: Three brothers married to three unrelated women; one brother dies and the second does Maamar then dies

micro_segments.title: The scenario: first brother dies, second brother does Maamar with first brother's wife, second brother dies
The problem: the first brother's widow has a double connection to the third brother (from two brothers)
Mishna rule: both women must do Chalitzah, not Yibum, due to the ambiguous status of Maamar
Rebbi Shimon's position: Maamar is either fully valid or invalid, creating a different analysis

macro_segments.title: Gemara's reframing: the double woman case as a Gezeira (preventive rabbinic ruling) not a Doraita problem

micro_segments.title: If the problem were Doraita, the first brother's widow would simply be excluded, needing no Chalitzah
The Gezeira reason: to avoid confusion, both women must do Chalitzah (Gezeira Shema Yomru two wives from one brother)
Why not compromise with Yibum and Chalitzah: fear people will do them in wrong order

macro_segments.title: Can the second brother give a Get to negate his Maamar and free the third brother for the other widow?

micro_segments.title: Rava's opinion: a Get to negate Maamar frees the co-wife but confuses the Maamar wife's status
The confusion: the Maamar wife looks like she received a Get to the Zika, preventing all Yibum
Alternative view: the Get only negates Maamar, not Zika, allowing the Maamar wife still to be available
Practical uncertainty: how to distinguish between Get to Maamar and Get to Zika

macro_segments.title: Two brothers married to two sisters; one brother dies, then his wife dies (Achos Isha case)

micro_segments.title: The scenario: sisters married to two brothers, first brother dies (wife becomes Achos Isha to second brother)
The first brother's widow is forbidden as Achos Isha; when second brother's wife dies, widow remains forbidden forever
Gemara's obvious question: this case is clearly forbidden, unlike the previous case where relationship continued
Why teach a case that is obvious: because the Mishna first taught the case where relationship continued, establishing the principle

topical_tags.term: Abaye vs. Rava (dispute over learning cases)
Rebbi Shimon (opposing view on Maamar validity)
Mipichem v'lo mipi Ksavam (testimony from memory not document)
